steps.title: Follow the contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts tragedies, which address broad themes that still appeal to audiences, with history plays, which can require knowledge of centuries of English history. "Consequently" signals the result of that contrast.

steps.title: Complete the logical result
steps.content: Choice B introduces twentieth-century plays, which the text never mentions. Choice C overstates Romeo and Juliet as the most accessible tragedy, and choice D invents experts' preferences. The remaining choice follows logically: modern audiences may find history plays less engaging than tragedies because the history plays require specialized historical knowledge. The answer is A.
